rulings.Case Title: KATY CHMURA VS. LAWRENCE CHOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Sever And Have Cross Complainants Causes Of Action Tried Separately And To Stay Action Pending Resolution Of The Main Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 31, 2015, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JAKE NG, STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS Notice Of Motion To Sever And Have Cross Complainants Causes Of Action Tried Separately And To Stay Action Pending Resolution Of The Main Action is continued to January 22, 2015 to be heard after cross-defendant's response to the first amended cross-complaint is filed. =(501/RAW)

rulings.Case Title: CORAL PARTNERS, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. SANJAY L PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Unlawful Detainer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 31, 2015, Line 2 ( Part 1 of 2). DEFENDANT SANJAY PATEL, RINA PATEL Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Unlawful Detainer is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and show that allegations in the complaint are false, irrelevant and improper. Motion to Strike is not a proper vehicle to challenge the sufficiency of the allegations in the complaint. Also, see ruling on demurrer. =(501/RAW)

rulings.Case Title: CORAL PARTNERS, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. SANJAY L PATEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 31, 2015, Line 2 ( Part 2 of 2). DEFENDANT SANJAY PATEL, RINA PATEL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party failed to show that this Court lacks jurisdiction. Charles J. Rounds Co. v. Joint Council of Teamsters (1971) 4 Cal.3d 888 is inapposite as defendants did not demur on the grounds of failure to exhaust arbitration remedies. First Amended Complaint states sufficient facts to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer. First Amended Complaint's verification is sufficient. See Claiborne v. Castle (1893) 98 Cal.30, 33. =(501/RAW)
